items.title: PEEK: Context Map as an Orientation Cache for Long-Context LLM Agents
items.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly operate over long and recurring external contexts, like document corpora and code repositories. Across invocations, existing approaches preserve either the agent's trajectory, passive access to raw material, or task-level strategies. None of them preserves what we argue is most needed for repeated same-context workloads: reusable orientation knowledge (e.g., what the context contains, how it is organized, and which entities, constants, and schemas have historically been useful) about the recurring context itself. We introduce PEEK, a system that caches and maintains this orientation knowledge as a context map: a small, constant-sized artifact in the agent's prompt that gives it a persistent peek into the external context. The map is maintained by a programmable cache policy with three modules: a Distiller that extracts transferable knowledge from inference-time signals, a Cartographer that translates it into structured edits, and a priority-based Evictor that enforces a fixed token budget. On long-context reasoning and information aggregation, PEEK improves over strong baselines by 6.3-34.0% while using 93-145 fewer iterations and incurring 1.7-5.8x lower cost than the state-of-the-art prompt-learning framework, ACE. On context learning, PEEK improves solving rate and rubric accuracy by 6.0-14.0% and 7.8-12.1%, respectively, at 1.4x lower cost than ACE. These gains generalize across LMs and agent architectures, including OpenAI Codex, a production-grade coding agent. Together, these results show that a context map helps long-context LLM agents interact with recurring external contexts more accurately and efficiently.
items.summary_cn:
## 文章做了什么
这篇论文研究长期上下文场景下的 LLM Agent 记忆问题。作者认为，现有方法要么保留轨迹，要么保留原始上下文，要么保留任务策略，但都没有稳定保留“关于上下文本身的可复用定向知识”。为此，论文提出 PEEK，用一个固定大小的 context map 作为缓存，把外部上下文中的结构、实体、常用常量和历史有效线索整理成可持续维护的提示工件，并通过 Distiller、Cartographer 和 Evictor 三个模块进行抽取、编辑和预算控制。

## 文章的核心贡献点
核心贡献是把长期上下文中的“定向知识”单独提升为一等对象，而不是把记忆等同于历史轨迹压缩。PEEK 的设计重点不在增加更多上下文，而在以可控缓存策略维护一个常量规模的知识地图，从而让 Agent 在反复访问同一代码库或文档集合时更快定位信息。

## 实现与部署，evaluation 结果，要有和相关工作对比
论文在长上下文推理、信息聚合与 context learning 任务上验证了 PEEK，相比强基线有 6.3% 到 34.0% 的提升，同时比 ACE 使用更少迭代并带来更低成本。作者还报告其方法可泛化到不同模型和 Agent 架构，包括生产级 coding agent。

#### 总结
PEEK 的价值在于把长期上下文 Agent 的记忆问题，从“多保留历史”转成“维护可复用上下文地图”，这让它在重复访问同一外部知识环境时更高效，也更接近真正可运维的 Agent 记忆机制。
items.summary_en:
## What This Paper Does
This paper studies memory for LLM agents operating over recurring long-context environments such as code repositories and document corpora. The authors argue that prior approaches preserve trajectories, raw context access, or task-level strategies, but fail to preserve reusable orientation knowledge about the context itself. They propose PEEK, which maintains a constant-size context map that stores structured knowledge about what the context contains, how it is organized, and which entities or schemas are repeatedly useful.

## Core Contributions
The main contribution is the treatment of orientation knowledge as a first-class memory object. PEEK uses a programmable cache policy with three components: a Distiller to extract transferable signals, a Cartographer to convert them into structured edits, and an Evictor to maintain a fixed token budget. This yields a reusable prompt artifact rather than a growing transcript.

## Implementation and Evaluation
The paper reports gains of 6.3% to 34.0% on long-context reasoning and information aggregation tasks over strong baselines, while also reducing iteration count and cost versus ACE. It further reports improvements on context learning tasks and claims the approach transfers across multiple language models and agent architectures.

#### Summary
PEEK reframes long-context agent memory as maintaining a reusable map of the environment instead of merely preserving more history, which makes the proposal operationally compelling for repeated same-context workloads.
items.render_excerpt:
机构: OpenAI, MIT
PEEK 的价值在于把长期上下文 Agent 的记忆问题，从“多保留历史”转成“维护可复用上下文地图”，这让它在重复访问同一外部知识环境时更高效，也更接近真正可运维的 Agent 记忆机制。
items.render_excerpt_en:

PEEK reframes long-context agent memory as maintaining a reusable map of the environment instead of merely preserving more history, which makes the proposal operationally compelling for repeated same-context workloads.

items.title: AgentAtlas: Beyond Outcome Leaderboards for LLM Agents
items.abstract: Large language model agents now act on codebases, browsers, operating systems, calendars, files, and tool ecosystems, but the benchmarks used to evaluate them are fragmented: each emphasizes a different unit of measurement (final task success, tool-call validity, repeated-pass consistency, trajectory safety, or attack robustness). A line of 2024-2025 work has converged on the diagnosis that a single accuracy column is no longer the right unit of comparison for deployable agents. AgentAtlas extends this line of work with four components: (i) a six-state control-decision taxonomy (Act / Ask / Refuse / Stop / Confirm / Recover); (ii) a nine-category trajectory-failure taxonomy with two orthogonal hierarchical labels (primary_error_source, impact); (iii) a taxonomy-aware vs. taxonomy-blind methodology that measures how much of a model's apparent capability comes from the supervision in the prompt; and (iv) a benchmark-coverage audit mapping fifteen agent benchmarks against six behavioral axes. To demonstrate the methodology we run a small fixed eight-model set (1,342 generated items, four frontier closed and four open-weight) under both prompt modes. Removing the explicit label menu drops every model's trajectory accuracy by 14-40 pp to a tight 0.54-0.62 floor regardless of family, and no single model wins on all three of control accuracy, trajectory diagnosis, and tool-context utility retention. We treat the synthetic run as a measurement-protocol demonstration, not a benchmark release.
items.summary_cn:
## 文章做了什么
这篇论文讨论 LLM Agent 评测为什么不能再只看单一成功率榜单。作者指出，不同 benchmark 分别强调最终成功、轨迹一致性、工具调用有效性、安全性或鲁棒性，导致“谁更强”常常取决于评价维度，而不是能力本身。论文提出 AgentAtlas，试图把 Agent 评测从结果排行榜扩展到控制决策、轨迹失败类型和 benchmark 覆盖面的更细粒度分析。

## 文章的核心贡献点
论文给出一个六类控制决策 taxonomy、一个九类轨迹失败 taxonomy，以及 taxonomy-aware 与 taxonomy-blind 两种评价设定，用来衡量模型能力到底有多少来自显式标签菜单本身。除此之外，作者还对 15 个 Agent benchmark 做了覆盖度审计，把已有 benchmark 映射到六个行为轴上。

## 实现与部署，evaluation 结果，要有和相关工作对比
作者在一个固定的八模型集合上做方法展示，结果显示一旦移除显式标签菜单，所有模型的轨迹准确率都会下降 14 到 40 个百分点，并集中到较接近的低位区间。这说明很多表面能力其实依赖 prompt 中给出的监督结构，而不是模型天然具备的稳定控制能力。

#### 总结
AgentAtlas 的重点不是再造一个新榜单，而是把 Agent 评测从单值结果拓展成多维行为诊断框架。它的价值在于帮助理解模型是“真的会”，还是“被 prompt 扶着会”。
items.summary_en:
## What This Paper Does
This paper argues that LLM agent evaluation can no longer be reduced to a single outcome leaderboard. Existing benchmarks emphasize different units of analysis, including final success, trajectory consistency, tool-call validity, safety, and robustness. AgentAtlas proposes a broader evaluation framework that exposes these differences instead of collapsing them into one score.

## Core Contributions
The paper introduces a six-state control-decision taxonomy, a nine-category trajectory-failure taxonomy, and a methodology that compares taxonomy-aware and taxonomy-blind prompting. It also audits benchmark coverage by mapping fifteen agent benchmarks onto six behavioral axes.

## Implementation and Evaluation
On a fixed eight-model experimental set, the authors show that removing an explicit label menu reduces trajectory accuracy by 14 to 40 percentage points for every model and compresses performance into a much narrower range. This suggests that part of the apparent capability measured by current prompts comes from the supervision structure itself rather than stable agent competence.

#### Summary
AgentAtlas is valuable less as another benchmark and more as an evaluation diagnosis layer: it helps separate real control competence from prompt-induced measurement artifacts.
items.render_excerpt: AgentAtlas 的重点不是再造一个新榜单，而是把 Agent 评测从单值结果拓展成多维行为诊断框架。它的价值在于帮助理解模型是“真的会”，还是“被 prompt 扶着会”。
items.render_excerpt_en: AgentAtlas is valuable less as another benchmark and more as an evaluation diagnosis layer: it helps separate real control competence from prompt-induced measurement artifacts.

items.title: Multi-agent Collaboration with State Management
items.abstract: Recent advances in multi-agent systems have shown great potential for solving complex tasks. However, when multiple agents edit a shared codebase concurrently, their changes can silently conflict and inconsistent views lead to integration failures. Existing multi-agent systems address this through workspace isolation (e.g., one git worktree per agent), but this defers conflict resolution to a post-hoc merge step where recovery is expensive. In this paper, we propose STORM, i.e., STate-ORiented Management for multi-agent collaboration. Specifically, STORM manages agent states by mediating their interactions with the shared workspace, ensuring that each agent operates on a consistent view of the codebase and that conflicting edits are detected and resolved at write time. We evaluate STORM on Commit0 and PaperBench across multiple LLMs. STORM outperforms the git-worktree-based multi-agent baseline by +18.7 on Commit0-Lite and +1.4 on PaperBench, while achieving comparable or better cost efficiency. Combined with single-agent runs, STORM reaches highest scores of 87.6 and 78.2 on the two benchmarks respectively, suggesting that explicit state management is a more effective foundation for multi-agent collaboration than workspace isolation. STORM can also be plugged into any multi-agent system seamlessly.
items.summary_cn:
## 文章做了什么
这篇论文关注多 Agent 同时修改共享代码库时的协作问题。现有系统常通过给每个 Agent 分配独立 worktree 来隔离修改，但这会把冲突留到后期 merge 阶段，修复成本高。作者提出 STORM，把“状态管理”作为多 Agent 协作的核心机制，在写入时就协调 Agent 对共享工作区的访问，从而尽早发现和处理冲突。

## 文章的核心贡献点
STORM 的核心思想是让 Agent 协作建立在一致视图和显式状态调度上，而不是只依赖工作空间隔离。它不是简单改进 merge 流程，而是把状态仲裁前移到协作执行期，这使多 Agent 在共享代码环境中更像一个受控系统，而不是多个独立分支事后拼接。

## 实现与部署，evaluation 结果，要有和相关工作对比
论文在 Commit0 和 PaperBench 上评估 STORM，相比基于 git worktree 的多 Agent 基线，在 Commit0-Lite 上提升 18.7 分，在 PaperBench 上提升 1.4 分，并保持可比甚至更好的成本效率。结合 single-agent 结果后，作者报告总分可达到两个 benchmark 上的最好表现。

#### 总结
STORM 说明，多 Agent 编程协作的关键瓶颈不只是分工，而是共享状态管理。相比把冲突推迟到 merge 阶段，写时协调是一条更稳的工程路线。
items.summary_en:
## What This Paper Does
This paper studies collaboration among multiple agents editing a shared codebase. Prior systems often isolate agents in separate git worktrees, which postpones conflict handling until merge time. The authors propose STORM, a state-oriented management layer that mediates agent interaction with the shared workspace and detects conflicting edits at write time.

## Core Contributions
The main contribution is the shift from workspace isolation to explicit state management. STORM aims to guarantee a consistent view of the codebase for each agent and moves conflict detection earlier in the execution process rather than treating it as a downstream merge problem.

## Implementation and Evaluation
The paper evaluates STORM on Commit0 and PaperBench. It reports a +18.7 improvement on Commit0-Lite and a +1.4 improvement on PaperBench over a git-worktree-based multi-agent baseline, while maintaining competitive cost efficiency. Combined with single-agent runs, the system reaches the highest reported scores in the paper on both benchmarks.

#### Summary
STORM makes a strong case that the central systems problem in multi-agent coding is shared state coordination, not just agent decomposition, and that write-time mediation is more effective than deferred merge-time recovery.
items.render_excerpt: STORM 说明，多 Agent 编程协作的关键瓶颈不只是分工，而是共享状态管理。相比把冲突推迟到 merge 阶段，写时协调是一条更稳的工程路线。
items.render_excerpt_en: STORM makes a strong case that the central systems problem in multi-agent coding is shared state coordination, not just agent decomposition, and that write-time mediation is more effective than deferred merge-time recovery.
